title: Migrating from triggers to application logic

current_state.description: DB triggers compute audit + denormalized fields

target_state.code_sample: app does both writes in transaction

migration_strategy: Phase 1: App does the work alongside trigger
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. App-level transaction wraps writes.
2. During transition, trigger still fires; verify it's a no-op (idempotent).
3. Eventually remove.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-action

title: PHP to Node.js migration of a single endpoint
migration_strategy: Phase 1: Node endpoint built, identical contract
Phase 2: Shadow traffic to both, compare
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Per-endpoint strangler.
2. Shadow proves equivalence.
3. PHP route only deleted after 0% traffic for grace period.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-endpoint

title: NestJS 9 → 10
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Most breaking changes are minor.
2. Test suite is the safety net.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
-- NestJS 9 → 10
-- Phase 1: add compatible structure without changing reads.
ALTER TABLE target_table ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS new_value TEXT;

-- Phase 2: backfill in bounded chunks with progress tracking.
UPDATE target_table
SET new_value = legacy_value
WHERE new_value IS NULL
  AND id IN (SELECT id FROM target_table WHERE new_value IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT 1000);

-- Phase 3: dual-read/dual-write in app code, compare counts, then cut over.
-- Phase 4: remove legacy path only after telemetry shows zero legacy reads for one full release window.

-- Rollback: keep legacy column/path until validation passes; disable new writes with a feature flag.

title: Splitting denormalized table into normalized
migration_strategy: Phase 1: Backfill customers from distinct emails
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Customer table extracted.
2. Orders linked.
3. Historical email preserved as denorm 'at_time' column if needed for audit.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Per-pipeline migration; both run during transition.
2. Compare outputs.
3. Secrets migrated to GH Actions secrets.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-repo

title: Migrating from GraphQL v1 to v2 schema
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Two endpoints, two schemas.
2. Resolvers can share or diverge.
3. Client-driven migration.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-client
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single endpoint: hard to deprecate cleanly.

title: Switching from JSON to JSONB
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. ALTER TYPE JSON→JSONB rewrites table.
2. New column + dual-write.
3. GIN index for jsonb queries.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: Extract subcomponent from god component
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Tests preserve behavior.
2. Extract per logical section.
3. State lifting careful — don't break form composition.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: CQRS introduction for read-heavy domain

current_state.description: Single DB; reads slow because writes lock

target_state.description: Write path + read replica with projections

migration_strategy: Phase 4: Specific projections (denorm) for hot reads
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Read replica handles ad-hoc reads.
2. Projections (denormalized) for hot queries.
3. Write path stays on primary.
4. Lag monitoring + alerts.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-query
what_breaks_naive_approach: Strict consistency assumed but replica lag breaks.

title: Schema reorg via blue/green databases
migration_strategy: Phase 1: Build green DB with new schema
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Blue (current) + Green (new) coexist.
2. ETL one-time + ongoing.
3. Cutover when green caught up.
feature_flag_strategy: DB endpoint

title: Cross-DB joins to denormalized projections

current_state.description: App joins users + orders + items across services

migration_strategy: Phase 1: View built from event consumption
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Event-driven projection.
2. Eventually consistent.
3. Rebuild from event log if corruption.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-read

title: Multi-cloud migration
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Multi-cloud is hard; consider whether truly needed.
2. Container portability.
3. Managed services tied to cloud (avoid for portable workloads).
feature_flag_strategy: Per-region

title: Lodash → native ES
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Direct sed (semantics differ for _.get on undefined paths etc.)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Most lodash has native equivalents.
2. _.get, _.cloneDeep are exceptions — keep targeted packages.
3. Per-function migration; not all-or-nothing.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
-- Lodash → native ES
-- Phase 1: add compatible structure without changing reads.
ALTER TABLE target_table ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS new_value TEXT;

-- Phase 2: backfill in bounded chunks with progress tracking.
UPDATE target_table
SET new_value = legacy_value
WHERE new_value IS NULL
  AND id IN (SELECT id FROM target_table WHERE new_value IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT 1000);

-- Phase 3: dual-read/dual-write in app code, compare counts, then cut over.
-- Phase 4: remove legacy path only after telemetry shows zero legacy reads for one full release window.

-- Rollback: keep legacy column/path until validation passes; disable new writes with a feature flag.
what_breaks_naive_approach: _.get with default value lost; subtle bugs.

title: Adding check constraint with backfill

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. NOT VALID adds constraint without scan.
2. VALIDATE scans without blocking writes.

title: Sharding a table by user_id
migration_strategy: Phase 1: Shard strategy decided (hash of user_id mod N)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Shard key chosen for query patterns.
2. App-level routing.
3. Cross-shard joins forbidden.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-user
what_breaks_naive_approach: No shard key planning: re-shard needed.

title: Splitting massive switch statement
migration_strategy: Phase 2: Extract each case to handler
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Each handler tested in isolation.
2. Registry map is data.
3. Open/closed.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
telemetry_requirements: Emit rollout, error-rate, latency, adoption, rollback, and user-impact metrics with enough tags to compare old and new paths.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Big switch grows; new contributors confused.

title: Splitting frontend per-route
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. React.lazy + Suspense.
2. Router-level splits.
3. Prefetch on hover for hot links.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
const Dashboard = lazy(() => import('./Dashboard'));
const Settings = lazy(() => import('./Settings'));

<Suspense fallback={<Spinner/>}>
  <Routes>
    <Route path="/dashboard" element={<Dashboard/>}/>
  </Routes>
</Suspense>

title: Adopting GDPR right-to-erasure

current_state.description: Customer data scattered; no erasure flow

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Catalog every place PII lives (DB, S3, logs, backups).
2. Erasure script touches all.
3. Backups: cannot erase; expire faster.
4. Anonymize where deletion breaks integrity.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
